sanskrit:
मयाध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम्।
हेतुनानेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते॥
transliteration:
mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ sūyate sa-carācaram
hetunānena kaunteya jagad viparivartate
literal_meaning: By Me as overseer, material-nature brings-forth the moving-and-non-moving; by this cause, O Kaunteya, the universe revolves.
contextual_meaning: The psyche’s automatic processes (prakṛti) run on their own, yet they require the silent presence of pure awareness (puruṣa) to function; without that witnessing presence, no thought, feeling or action can arise.
psychological_state: Arjuna experiences a moment of ego-softening: the burden of personal authorship lightens as he realizes the mind-body system operates under a higher-order witnessing intelligence.
trigger_scenario: You’re stuck in rumination at 2 a.m., believing you must fix every thought. Remembering this verse, you shift from controller to observer; the mental movie keeps playing, but the watcher is untouched.
anchor_text: Let life unfold while you quietly witness; your still presence lets every thought and action arise.
